content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN integers, and an integer KKK such that 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§K1 \le A_i \le K1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§K, and each integer 1,2‚Ä¶,K1, 2 \ldots, K1,2‚Ä¶,K appears at least once in the array AAA.

Suppose you start at some position SSS (1‚Č§S‚Č§N1 \le S \le N1‚Č§S‚Č§N), solve the following problem:


A sequence of (K+1)(K + 1)(K+1) integers, B0,B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BKB_0, B_1, B_2, \ldots, B_{K}B0‚Äč,B1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BK‚Äč is called good if it satisfies the following conditions:

B0=SB_0 = SB0‚Äč=S
1‚Č§Bi‚Č§N1 \le B_i \le N1‚Č§Bi‚Äč‚Č§N for each 1‚Č§i‚Č§K1 \le i \le K1‚Č§i‚Č§K
ABi=iA_{B_i} = iABi‚Äč‚Äč=i for each 1‚Č§i‚Č§K1 \le i \le K1‚Č§i‚Č§K


Among all good sequences, find the minimum possible value of ‚ąĎi=1K‚ą£Bi‚ąíBi‚ąí1‚ą£\sum_{i = 1}^{K} |B_i - B_{i - 1}|‚ąĎi=1K‚Äč‚ą£Bi‚Äč‚ąíBi‚ąí1‚Äč‚ą£.
Less formally, we want a sequence starting at SSS and then the next element is an occurrence of 111, the next an occurrence of 222, and so on till an occurrence of KKK, and we should find the sequence that minimizes the total distance.

The starting position SSS is not fixed, so you need to print the answer for each S=1,2,‚Ä¶,NS = 1, 2, \ldots, NS=1,2,‚Ä¶,N.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains 222 integers - NNN and KKK, the size of the array and the upper bound on the elements.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN integers - the answers if we started our sequence at SSS for each S=1,2,‚Ä¶,NS = 1, 2, \ldots, NS=1,2,‚Ä¶,N in this order.
constraints: For each X(1‚Č§X‚Č§K)X (1 \le X \le K)X(1‚Č§X‚Č§K) there exists iii such that Ai=XA_i = XAi‚Äč=X.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
